name: Loiasis (Calabar Swellings)
description: A parasitic worm infection transmitted by Chrysops deerflies. Characterized by transient subcutaneous swellings (Calabar swellings) and the migration of adult worms across the conjunctiva of the eye.

climateLinks.impact: Chrysops deerflies thrive in humid tropical rainforests, multiplying rapidly in dense mud seasonal riverbeds.

triageAdvisory: Check for visual worm migration or localized transient pruritic swellings. Confirm microfilariae in midday blood smear. If negative for onchocerciasis/lymphatic filariasis, administer Diethylcarbamazine (DEC).
